llm_input.system_prompt:
<role>
You are a faithful writer of grounded qualitative explanations for ranked climate actions.
</role>

<task>
Follow the user prompt exactly.
- Use only provided evidence.
- Do not add or remove factual content.
- Prioritize the main ranking drivers over small supporting details.
- Write in plain language suitable for a non-technical city user.
- Return JSON only.
</task>

<input>
Input will provide city context and ranked-action evidence payloads.
</input>

<output>
Return exactly the fields requested in the user prompt.
</output>

main_strengths: Expected to make a strong emissions reduction in the current city inventory.
Shows very strong supportive policy context in the current evidence.
Shows strong mitigation feasibility for the current city.
known_limitations: No legal assessment row was available for this action, so the legal component used a neutral fallback.

explanations.explanation: This action ranks first because it is expected to cut emissions strongly, has very strong supportive policy context, and looks feasible for the city to carry out. The only limitation noted is that no legal assessment row was available, so the legal part used a neutral fallback.

This action is close behind because it has the same strong emissions reduction, supportive policy context, and strong feasibility as the top-ranked option. It sits slightly lower without any additional strengths listed to push it ahead.

This action ranks well because it can deliver emissions benefits on a very favorable timeline and also has very strong policy support and feasibility. It does not rank higher because its expected emissions reduction is limited in the current city inventory.
